title: senna racemosa var. liebmannii
intro: Senna racemosa var. liebmannii, also known as Mexican senna, is a flowering plant in the legume family that is native to Mexico and Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including being a natural laxative due to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate the bowels and promote bowel movements. The health benefits of Senna racemosa var. liebmannii include relief from constipation, and it has also been used to treat other gastrointestinal issues such as irritable bowel syndrome. In herbal preparations, Senna racemosa var. liebmannii is often used as a dried leaf or root, and can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules for medicinal use.
